ip_entries.title: Closed-Loop Intelligence

content.origin: Base TMR. Decision 23 (three flywheels) is the architectural descendant.
content.rationale: Conceptual ancestor of the three-flywheel architecture locked as Decision 23. Cited in Addenda 1, 4, 6, 9, 10. The meta-principle is older and broader than any single flywheel. Terminology evolved from 'training signal' to 'classification signal' (Meta-E finding) but the commitment is stable.
content.statement: Every interaction generates a classification signal that accumulates into the platform's learning systems. The three-flywheel architecture (Tenant, Foundational, Domain) is the structural expression of this commitment.
cross_references: Meta-E (training signal → classification signal terminology drift)
content.drift_assessment: HIGH for terminology (training signal vs classification signal across documents). LOW-MEDIUM for the principle itself.

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'Every interaction generates a classification signal that accumulates into the platform's learning systems. The three-flywheel architecture (Tenant, Foundational, Domain) is the structural expression of this commitment.' The audit evidence shows classification_signals is write-only (convergence service has zero .select calls against it), the flywheel tables (foundational_patterns, domain_patterns, structural_fingerprints) operate as bypass channels reading/writing outside classification_signals, and the signal surface lacks run_id for within-run filtering. This directly implicates the closed-loop intelligence commitment: if signals are written but never read back, the loop is not closed. The G11 ABSENT verdict on both within-run and cross-run read-paths is a structural violation of this principle's core proposition that signals 'accumulate into the platform's learning systems.'

ip_entries.title: Carry Everything, Express Contextually

content.origin: Addendum 1, CLT-14B / OB-27B. Born from $392,577 underpayment caused by AI-as-filter pattern.
content.rationale: Core architectural principle already in memory with verbatim consistency. Cited as alignment in 4+ addenda. Predates Decision 51. The pattern shows up at multiple platform levels (Three-Layer Data Architecture, Semantic Resolution Hierarchy, Convergence as Observation), suggesting it is a governing principle, not merely an engineering method.
content.statement: At import time, the platform persists ALL data regardless of whether AI has classified it. AI classifications are hints, not gates. Data is preserved at import; context activates at calculation time.
content.drift_assessment: LOW as principle. MEDIUM at scope edges — Addendum 6 extends to live/official/projection windows.

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'At import time, the platform persists ALL data regardless of whether AI has classified it. AI classifications are hints, not gates. Data is preserved at import; context activates at calculation time.' The audit evidence reveals that classification-relevant data is scattered across 4 whole-table bypass channels (synaptic_density, foundational_patterns, domain_patterns, structural_fingerprints) plus 2 JSONB per-key bypasses (import_batches.metadata.classification, import_batches.metadata.contentUnitId). The 'Carry Everything' principle requires all data to be persisted and contextually activated — but the bypass channels mean classification intelligence is carried in fragmented locations rather than through a single canonical surface. The principle's 'Express Contextually' dimension is structurally impossible when the signal surface itself cannot be queried for within-run context (no run_id column).

ip_entries.title: Principle-Rule Coherence and the Supersession Surface

content.origin: Session 2026-04-13, Q7 IRA invocation against loop integration design surface. Discovered when IRA returned faithful applicability brief but did not surface that E18 narrowed higher-tier principles rather than instantiating them.
content.statement: A substrate-bound governance system serves principles through rules. Rules instantiate principles for specific work classes; principles supply the standing intent the rules are supposed to enact. When the substrate is queried for binding intelligence on a piece of work, two distinct findings are possible: (1) applicability — which rules govern this work — and (2) coherence — whether the rules at lower tiers still serve the principles at higher tiers, given the specific case under consideration. Applicability is rule-level; coherence is principle-level. Applicability without coherence produces under-service: the rule was applied, the work conformed, the principle was nonetheless degraded because the rule no longer instantiates the principle for the case at hand. This principle establishes that principle-rule coherence is a first-class IRA concern, on equal standing with applicability. It establishes that supersession-of-rules-by-principle is a first-class outcome of governance reasoning, not an exceptional event. It establishes that the IGF agents must operationalize the Innovate dimension by contracting a surface where coherence findings flow to architect disposition through the same structured path that applicability findings already do.
implications: IRA output contract gains supersession_candidates field
IRA operating loop gains coherence-check step
IVA contract may need parallel extension
Tier 0 entries subject to coherence questioning via bootstrap modification protocol

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'When the substrate is queried for binding intelligence on a piece of work, two distinct findings are possible: (1) applicability — which rules govern this work — and (2) coherence — whether the rules at lower tiers still serve the principles at higher tiers, given the specific case under consideration.' This audit IS a coherence review: the architect is asking whether the lower-tier implementation (convergence service, signal surface schema, flywheel tables) still serves the higher-tier principles (G7, G11) given the code-and-schema evidence collected. The entry further states: 'Applicability without coherence produces under-service: the rule was applied, the work conformed, the principle was nonetheless degraded because the rule no longer instantiates the principle for the case at hand.' The evidence shows exactly this pattern — classification_signals exists (rule applied), writes occur (work conforms to write-path), but the principle (read-path coherence, single canonical surface) is degraded because the implementation does not instantiate the principle.

ip_entries.title: IRA Returns Recommendations, Not Just Bindings

content.origin: Session 2026-04-13. Discovered when architect-channel IRA invocation (Q7-class architectural design question) returned faithful applicability brief but did not evaluate the three implementation-surface options (CLI extension, runtime hook, claude.ai integration) against substrate. Architect named the gap: Shouldn't IRA be able to report back a path based upon the logic and cross evaluation with an option that best aligns to the substrate? Confirmed: limiting IRA to user-supplied options only would suppress LLM intelligence that the system was built to use.
content.statement: When a piece of work includes options — either user-supplied (the architect names candidate paths in the work description) or substrate-grounded (substrate explicitly enumerates alternatives that apply to the work) — IRA evaluates each option against the assembled brief and surfaces a ranked recommendation with substrate-cited reasoning per option. IRA self-generates options when substrate names them as alternatives, exercising the LLM intelligence that substrate-bound reasoning makes available; suppressing this would waste the intelligence the system was built to use. IRA does not invent options not present in either source. The recommendation field is empty when no substrate-grounded options exist for evaluation; an empty array is correct output, not failure. The architect retains disposition authority on the recommendation; IRA recommends, the architect decides.
implications: IRA prompt template (T0-E09) gains operating loop step (Step 8 — Option Evaluation Against Brief) and output contract field (option_recommendations: Array of {option_id, source (user_supplied | substrate_named), substrate_basis (entry_ids that ground the option), alignment_reasoning, conflicts_detected, recommendation_rank, recommended_action}).

The architect role compresses from make the call given partial substrate-served information to review IRA substrate-grounded recommendation, override if engineering judgment differs, otherwise proceed.
Architect bandwidth shifts toward genuinely novel decisions that substrate cannot serve.
cross_references: IGF-T0-E09 (IRA Prompt Template — extension target via subsequent HF-IGF-03)
IGF-T0-E16 (Agent Boundary Protocol — IRA recommends, never modifies)

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'When a piece of work includes options — either user-supplied (the architect names candidate paths in the work description) or substrate-grounded (substrate explicitly enumerates alternatives that apply to the work) — IRA evaluates each option against the assembled brief and surfaces a ranked recommendation with substrate-cited reasoning per option.' The architect explicitly names two options: (a) remediable through architectural pattern change and (b) requires rebuild. These are user-supplied options requiring Step 8 evaluation. The entry further states: 'IRA does not invent options not present in either source. The recommendation field is empty when no substrate-grounded options exist for evaluation.' Both options are present in the user prompt; evaluation is required.

ip_entries.title: Closed-Loop Learning

content.one_line: Platform activity generates training signals for continuous improvement; manual corrections inform future AI interpretation.
content.statement: Platform activity generates training signals for continuous improvement. AI mappings that get manually corrected inform future AI interpretation. The platform gets smarter with use. Every correction, override, or manual adjustment is a learning signal that must feed back into the AI interpretation layer, not be discarded after the immediate fix. Closed-loop learning is the mechanism by which the platform compounds intelligence over time; without it, every correction is a one-time patch rather than a permanent improvement.

provenance.origin_section: SECTION A: DESIGN PRINCIPLES (NON-NEGOTIABLE) — 5. Closed-Loop Learning

applies_when: Designing AI mapping or interpretation pipelines
Implementing manual correction or override flows
Evaluating whether a feature captures learning signals from user actions
Reviewing whether corrections feed back into future AI behavior
Assessing whether a delivered AI feature has a feedback path
escalates_when: A pattern of repeated identical corrections is found with no evidence of AI improvement over time
A correction flow is delivered without any feedback path and the gap is not documented as a known limitation
cross_references: Be the Thermostat, Not the Thermometer (CC Standing Rule)
adherence_patterns: Every manual correction or override flow includes a capture step that records the correction as a learning signal
AI interpretation pipelines have documented feedback paths from correction events
Feature design for correction flows explicitly identifies how the signal reaches the AI layer

violation_patterns.description: Manual corrections or overrides are applied but not captured as training signals for future AI interpretation
A correction flow is implemented with no feedback path back to the AI interpretation layer
AI mapping pipeline is designed as one-way with no mechanism to incorporate user corrections

false_positive_triggers: One-time data corrections for genuinely corrupt ingested data are not learning signals and need not feed back into AI interpretation

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'Every correction, override, or manual adjustment is a learning signal that must feed back into the AI interpretation layer, not be discarded after the immediate fix. Closed-loop learning is the mechanism by which the platform compounds intelligence over time; without it, every correction is a one-time patch rather than a permanent improvement.' The audit evidence shows the convergence service writes to classification_signals but never reads from it (zero .select calls). The flywheel pipeline reads from foundational_patterns and domain_patterns — bypass channels — not from classification_signals. This means the closed-loop learning mechanism bypasses the canonical signal surface entirely. The principle's core proposition — that the platform 'compounds intelligence over time' — is structurally undermined when the signal surface is write-only and the learning loop reads from bypass channels.

ip_entries.title: Closed-Loop Intelligence Applied to Data Ingestion

content.one_line: Every ingestion event strengthens classification signals, creating an ML flywheel where more data leads to better classification, less human intervention, and faster processing.
content.statement: Closed-Loop Intelligence (IGF-T1-E906) applied to data ingestion means that every file or transaction that arrives and is classified — whether by human confirmation or autonomous processing — strengthens the classification signals for that tenant's data patterns. The ML flywheel spins: more data → better classification → less human intervention → faster processing → more data. The first file requires full human review. By the tenth file with the same structure, classification confidence approaches 1.0 and ingestion is fully autonomous. Human involvement only resurfaces when the data structure changes. This is not a static model trained once — it is a continuously improving system where operational use is the training signal. Any implementation that discards classification outcomes (human or autonomous) without feeding them back into the signal accumulation mechanism breaks the flywheel. The flywheel is the mechanism by which the platform's value compounds over time; breaking it reduces the platform to a static classification tool.
applies_when: Evaluating the classification pipeline's learning architecture
Measuring the reduction in human intervention over successive ingestion events
Designing the signal accumulation and confidence boosting mechanism
Assessing whether a new ingestion feature preserves or breaks the flywheel
Reviewing whether human classification confirmations are being captured as training signals
Designing the feedback path from human review decisions back into the classification model
escalates_when: Human intervention rate for a tenant is not declining after 20+ ingestion events with the same data structure, indicating the signal accumulation mechanism may be broken
Classification confidence scores are not increasing over successive ingestion events for a stable data structure
A schema change causes the flywheel to reset but the platform does not detect the schema change, leading to incorrect autonomous classifications
cross_references: Autonomous Classification via Accumulated Confidence Signals
Schema Change Detection Failure in Autonomous Processing
All Ingestion Channels Produce Identical Committed Data Records
adherence_patterns: Every classification outcome — human or autonomous — is written to the signal accumulation store with the tenant, data domain, file structure fingerprint, and confidence score
The classification model's confidence for a given tenant and data domain increases monotonically as more signals accumulate for that pattern
Human intervention rate is tracked per tenant and reported in the Operate workspace as a flywheel health metric
Signal accumulation is per-tenant and isolated — one tenant's signals cannot influence another's classification model
The flywheel health metric triggers an alert if human intervention rate is not declining after a defined number of ingestion events

violation_patterns.description: Human classification confirmations are not fed back into the signal accumulation mechanism, breaking the flywheel's learning loop
The classification model is retrained only on a fixed schedule rather than continuously as new signals arrive, introducing lag in the flywheel
Classification signals are accumulated globally rather than per-tenant, causing one tenant's data patterns to pollute another's classification model
A new ingestion channel (e.g., API push) does not feed classification outcomes into the same signal accumulation mechanism as SFTP, creating a split flywheel
The platform does not track the reduction in human intervention rate over time, making it impossible to verify that the flywheel is spinning

does_not_apply_when: Classification of entirely novel data domains with no prior signals — the flywheel requires at least one prior classification event to begin accumulating
One-time historical data loads that will not recur and therefore cannot benefit from accumulated signals
false_positive_triggers: A tenant whose data structure changes frequently will show a persistently high human intervention rate — this is correct flywheel behavior, not a broken flywheel; the flywheel resets for new structures
A new tenant's high human intervention rate in the first weeks is expected flywheel startup behavior, not a failure

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'The ML flywheel spins: more data → better classification → less human intervention → faster processing → more data.' And: 'Any implementation that discards classification outcomes (human or autonomous) without feeding them back into the signal accumulation mechanism breaks the flywheel.' The audit evidence (S-CODE-G11-02) shows flywheel files (flywheel-pipeline.ts, fingerprint-flywheel.ts) have zero references to classification_signals. The flywheel reads/writes go to foundational_patterns, domain_patterns, structural_fingerprints — NOT classification_signals. This is a structural bypass: the flywheel accumulation mechanism does not consume the canonical signal surface. The principle's violation pattern '[high] A new ingestion channel (e.g., API push) does not feed classification outcomes into the same signal accumulation mechanism as SFTP, creating a split flywheel' applies by analogy — the flywheel and the signal surface are split.

ip_entries.title: IRA (Intelligence Resolution Agent) Prompt Template

content.name: IRA Prompt Template

health.verification_evidence: OB-IGF-26 R1 Phase C + D revision. Evidence: DS-021 v2 §4 signals + §12 EECI; OB-IGF-26 R0 Phase B halt diagnostic (second reduction layer in src/lib/agents/ira/passes/extract.ts); R1 Phase C replaces Pass 1 / Pass 2 with src/lib/agents/ira/reasoning.ts::reasonAgainstSurface; R1 Phase D retires fidelityAtInitialFilter helper; 82/82 IRA tests pass; Phase E validation invocation observed under revised runtime per OB-IGF-26 R1 §2 Recusal Gate (architect is evaluator).

content.purpose: Given a description of intended work, return the precise applicable intelligence as a structured brief.
content.one_line: Given a description of intended work, return the precise applicable intelligence as a structured brief.
content.statement: The Intelligence Resolution Agent (IRA) is the pre-work agent. Given a description of intended work, the IRA returns the precise applicable intelligence as a structured brief. The brief is what the user works against — it is not a recommendation, it is a constraint. CC, design conversations, and Andrew himself work against the brief and can refer to it during work as the authoritative source of 'what intelligence applies here.'

historical_statements.wording: Original v1 — operating loop with 6 steps, output contract without supersession_candidates
v2 (HF-IGF-01) — added Step 7 (Principle-Rule Coherence Check) and supersession_candidates output field

applies_when: Modifying the IRA prompt template
Reviewing IRA brief output for correctness
Designing IRA invocation points in the integration model
Tuning IRA performance metrics (precision, recall, latency)
Auditing whether IRA is conforming to its boundary (read-only on substrate)
Modifying the IRA prompt builder or the SurfaceManifest-to-prompt formatting
Adding or changing substrate body fields that the prompt should surface inline
Reviewing whether the reasoning plane receives substrate body at the intended fidelity
Modifying the unified reasoning call (src/lib/agents/ira/reasoning.ts) or its tool schemas
Changing the CRL-governed strategy selection (single vs decomposed) in runtime.ts
Revising signal-fidelity resolution or the SurfaceManifest fidelity-to-signal mapping
Reviewing whether new runtime mechanics preserve Pass 3 assemble.ts fail-loud invariants
escalates_when: IRA cannot determine applicability with sufficient confidence
Substrate has no entries covering the work area (gap detection)
Multiple high-priority entries conflict and resolution requires human judgment

input_contract.work_type: ob | hf | ds | clt | design_question | memory_edit | igf_modification | other
input_contract.work_scope: Optional hints to narrow resolution: touches_auth, touches_calculation_engine, touches_committed_data, touches_ui, touches_ingestion, touches_schema, touches_governance, other_scope_hints
input_contract.work_description: Free-form natural language description of intended work. Specific enough that applicability can be reasoned about, but not so detailed that it duplicates the implementation.

operating_loop.name: Prior art retrieval
operating_loop.description: Compute governance fingerprint from input contract (task_class + work_scope + initial substrate-binding-signature-estimate + output_class per DA-IGF-02 §3). Query L3 igf.prior_art_density at three flywheel scopes in priority order: conversation > project > domain. Tier 1 exact match: return cached brief; skip Steps 1-8. Tier 2 structural analogy: inherit cached binding signature; execute Steps 5-8 only with targeted LLM. Tier 3 novel: proceed Steps 1-8 fully; ICA captures novel fingerprint to L3 post-invocation. Step 0 read-only on substrate via igf_ira_service.

operating_loop.name: Surface Judgment
operating_loop.description: Between Step 0 density lookup and Pass 1 Identify, the Surface is classified for substrate-binding properties: work_scope density, task_class signal strength, substrate-binding breadth, Surface assembly health. This is a deterministic classification pass; any LLM-assisted sub-step inherits Step 0 deterministic authority and cannot override Step 0 tier_verdict. Step 0.5 produces a SurfaceJudgment packet consumed by Pass 1.
operating_loop.output_contract: SurfaceJudgment packet: judgment_verdict (proceed/narrow/abort), substrate_binding_density, work_scope_alignment, anomaly_flags
operating_loop.permitted_short_circuit: judgment_verdict=abort returns immediately with preamble_text citing reason; no Pass 1-3 executed

operating_loop.name: Substrate search
operating_loop.description: Tier-aware search using work description, work type, and scope hints. Always loads Tier 0 (Bootstrap), Tier 1 (Governing Principles), and any explicitly tagged scope tiers. Performs LLM-assisted semantic search across remaining tiers using applies_when and does_not_apply_when descriptors. NOT a vector search alone.

operating_loop.name: Applicability reasoning
operating_loop.description: For each candidate entry, reason about whether the entry actually applies to this specific work. Reasoning is logged as part of the brief — the user can see why each entry was included and why others were excluded.

operating_loop.name: Priority ranking
operating_loop.description: Applicable entries are ranked. Tier 0 and Tier 1 always rank highest. Within tiers: directness of applicability, severity of associated violation patterns, recency of invocation, signal-strength evidence from prior IVA outcomes.

operating_loop.name: Cross-reference expansion
operating_loop.description: For each top-ranked entry, follow cross_references and include referenced entries that meet a relevance threshold. Prevents the failure mode where a brief includes Calculation Sovereignty but not Carry Everything.

operating_loop.name: Health check
operating_loop.description: Inspect the health metadata of every entry returned. Stale, drift-flagged, and SUPERSEDED-PENDING VERIFICATION entries are surfaced explicitly with the staleness/verification context.

operating_loop.name: Gap detection
operating_loop.description: Check whether the work touches a topic the substrate does not cover. If uncovered, produce an explicit POSSIBLE GAP finding rather than returning an empty brief.

operating_loop.name: Principle-rule coherence check
operating_loop.description:
For each high-tier principle entry returned in the brief (Tier 0 and Tier 1), examine whether the lower-tier rule entries also returned (Tier 2 through Tier 6) actually instantiate that principle for the work at hand, or whether the work surfaces a case the rules do not serve. For each detected incoherence, produce a supersession_candidates entry naming the rule whose service of the principle is in question, the governing principle, the aspect of the work that surfaces the under-service, and a recommended_action of extend, supersede, or reaffirm_as_exception. Coherence findings are observational, not prescriptive — IRA names the question; the architect dispositions the response. Coherence is checked against the work description, not against the abstract substrate state.

When Step 7 identifies a principle-rule under-service that is ALSO surfaced by Step 8 option evaluation (an option alignment_reasoning cites the same coherence), the finding populates BOTH surfaces: top-level supersession_candidates AND corresponding option_recommendations[i].coherence_findings. Step 7 and Step 8 are not mutually exclusive; an invocation may fire both. An LLM that populates one surface while having content for both violates the adherence pattern.

operating_loop.name: Option evaluation against brief
operating_loop.description:
When a piece of work includes options — either user-supplied (the architect names candidate paths in the work description) or substrate-grounded (substrate explicitly enumerates alternatives that apply to the work) — IRA evaluates each option against the assembled brief and produces a ranked recommendation with substrate-cited reasoning per option. IRA self-generates options when substrate names them as alternatives, exercising the LLM intelligence that substrate-bound reasoning makes available. IRA does not invent options not present in either source. Each option produces an entry in option_recommendations with fields per T1-E913 verbatim: option_id, source (user_supplied | substrate_named), substrate_basis (entry_ids that ground the option), alignment_reasoning, conflicts_detected, recommendation_rank, recommended_action. Per-option coherence_findings (when produced) propagate to top-level supersession_candidates only when the finding identifies a principle-rule under-service that applies cross-cutting; option-local findings (option conflicts with a specific entry) remain scoped to the option entry. The architect retains disposition authority on the recommendation; IRA recommends, the architect decides.

Option evaluation does not substitute for Step 7. If any option alignment_reasoning identifies principle-rule under-service, the coherence finding is surfaced in both option_recommendations[i].coherence_findings (option-local) AND top-level supersession_candidates (cross-cutting implication).

output_contract.tier_verdict: Enum: tier_1_exact_match | tier_2_structural_analogy | tier_3_novel. Required on every invocation. Fail-loud invariant.
output_contract.possible_gaps: Array of {description, recommended_action} for areas the substrate does not cover
output_contract.evaluation_status: Enum: 'did_not_fire' (Step 8 not invoked because no options present in work or substrate) | 'fired_with_results' (Step 8 invoked, option_recommendations populated) | 'fired_no_options' (Step 8 invoked but no substrate-grounded options found for evaluation). Honors T1-E906 closed-loop requirement that absence of result is distinguishable from failure to invoke.
output_contract.applicable_entries: Array of {id, tier, relevance, reasoning, health_warnings}
output_contract.excluded_candidates: Array of {id, reasoning} for entries considered but excluded
output_contract.retrieved_prior_art: Object {fingerprint_hash, scope_level, scope_identifier, match_count, confidence, brief_payload} on Tier 1/2; null on Tier 3.
output_contract.work_description_hash: sha256 of input — so IVA can verify it is verifying against the same input
output_contract.option_recommendations: Array of {option_id (string), source (user_supplied | substrate_named), substrate_basis ([entry_ids]), alignment_reasoning (string), conflicts_detected (Array of {conflicting_entry_id, conflict_type, reasoning}), recommendation_rank (integer, 1=highest), recommended_action (string), coherence_findings (Array, optional, option-local scope)} for each option evaluated. Empty array when Step 8 did not fire (no options present in work or substrate).
output_contract.supersession_candidates: Array of {entry_id, governing_principle_id, coherence_finding, recommended_action (extend | supersede | reaffirm_as_exception), reasoning} for rules that may under-serve the higher-tier principles they instantiate, given the specific work. Empty array when no under-service detected.
output_contract.required_actions_before_work: Array of actions the user should take before work begins (e.g., 'Verify GP-02 against current calculation engine')

adherence_patterns.description: Every IRA invocation produces a brief conforming to the output contract
IRA reasoning is logged for every entry included in the brief
IRA writes only to agent_invocations table
Stale entries are surfaced in brief health_warnings, not silently ignored
Every IRA brief evaluates principle-rule coherence for the specific work via Step 7 and surfaces findings in supersession_candidates, even when the array is empty (empty array confirms the check ran)

Step 8 fires when work description names options OR substrate-bound entries enumerate alternatives applicable to the work; option_recommendations populated with substrate-grounded ranking and reasoning per option
evaluation_status is set on every IRA invocation regardless of whether Step 8 fires, providing closed-loop signal per T1-E906
Every IRA invocation produces a Step 0 tier verdict before Steps 1-8 execute
Tier 1 cache hits return cached brief with cost_usd=0; Steps 1-8 do not execute
Tier 3 novel results trigger ICA capture of new prior_art_density row post-invocation
When Step 7 and Step 8 both identify the same principle-rule under-service, both output surfaces are populated; the LLM does not choose between them

Option_recommendations that propose architectural changes (new modules, new boundaries, new abstractions) include an explicit fitness-of-purpose assessment: what defect surface does the change address, and is the change proportionate to that surface? Options proposing architecture beyond defect-proportionality MUST carry this assessment in their alignment_reasoning.

violation_patterns.description: IRA prompt template modified without bootstrap modification protocol
IRA invoked with input that does not conform to the input contract
IRA output that does not conform to the output contract

violation_patterns.consequence: Boundary violation — IRA must remain read-only.
violation_patterns.description: IRA write to any substrate table other than agent_invocations

violation_patterns.consequence: Innovate dimension of E/E/C/I tetrad has no contracted surface; substrate accumulates institutionalized drift between principles and rules silently.
violation_patterns.description: IRA returns a rule as binding without examining whether it still serves the higher-tier principle it instantiates for the specific work (Step 7 omitted)
Step 7 produces a coherence finding but supersession_candidates output omits it (silent drop)

violation_patterns.consequence: T1-E913 not served; architect synthesizes recommendation without substrate grounding.
violation_patterns.description: IRA returns brief with options present in work description but option_recommendations field empty with evaluation_status='did_not_fire'

violation_patterns.consequence: T1-E913 boundary violated; IRA invents options not present in source.
violation_patterns.description: option_recommendations contains entries with option_id not traceable to either work_description or substrate entry IDs

violation_patterns.consequence: T1-E906 closed loop not served; architect cannot distinguish step-not-fired from step-fired-empty.
violation_patterns.description: evaluation_status field omitted from output
IRA Steps 1-8 execute without prior Step 0 tier check
IRA writes to igf.prior_art_density (boundary violation; ICA owns L3 INSERT per T0-E16)
Step 0 fingerprint computation skipped; substrate query runs without cache lookup

violation_patterns.consequence: Coherence/options collapse - observed in OB-IGF-15 Phase 1 pre-HF (2026-04-13). Architect loses the option ranking view OR the coherence view; dispositioning is degraded.
violation_patterns.description: An invocation where option_recommendations is empty while supersession_candidates contains findings that ranked options, OR vice versa

violation_patterns.consequence: Body-absent reasoning failure mode reemerges; the 2026-04-19 diagnostic repeats.
violation_patterns.description: Prompt builder reduces a full_body or density_summary entry to identifier-only before LLM consumption (reintroduces the retired Phase C shim pattern).

violation_patterns.consequence: Carry Everything violated at the prompt layer; reasoning plane cannot cite propositions the substrate actually contains.
violation_patterns.description: Prompt builder strips a field the fidelity level prescribes to include (e.g., dropping applies_when from a density_summary entry).

violation_patterns.consequence: Korean Test violated; non-English substrate cannot be surfaced uniformly.
violation_patterns.description: Prompt builder inserts substrate content by language-specific content matching rather than by structural field name / tier selection.

violation_patterns.consequence: Developer-assigned numerical value reintroduced; fidelity is no longer the assembler's structural decision.
violation_patterns.description: Prompt builder introduces a fidelity threshold beyond what the assembler assigned (e.g., a hardcoded token budget that skips entries below a numerical cutoff).

violation_patterns.consequence: Null-safety requirement violated; runtime halts on valid-but-variant substrate JSONB shapes.
violation_patterns.description: Prompt builder fails on shape variance (e.g., substrate entry missing one field throws rather than degrading gracefully).

violation_patterns.consequence: Second reduction layer reemerges; body-absent reasoning at Pass 2 returns; OB-IGF-26 R0 Phase B halt condition reproduces.
violation_patterns.description: Runtime reintroduces a Pass 1 / Pass 2 serial pipeline or any per-unit extractor that rebuilds prompts inline from entry_id + keyword-search surface.

violation_patterns.consequence: The retired Pass 1 -> Pass 2 message-passing pattern sneaks back under a different name. Branch calls must each receive the full manifest; no inter-branch wire-up.
violation_patterns.description: Decomposition path implemented with serial dependencies between branches (e.g., options call receives entries-branch output as input).

violation_patterns.consequence: No-developer-assigned-numerical-values rule violated. All strategy decisions must be CRL-read or tier-structural per DS-021 v2 §4.2.
violation_patterns.description: Hardcoded numerical threshold introduced in strategy selection (e.g., "> 25% failure rate" or "manifest entry count > N").

violation_patterns.consequence: Signal stream misrepresents what the reasoning plane saw. CRL accumulation at the wrong fidelity produces spurious strategy decisions.
violation_patterns.description: Signal writer records fidelity_level from a constant or tier-default rather than from the manifest entry actually delivered to the prompt.

violation_patterns.consequence: T1-E903 tier-verdict authority, prior-art fingerprint authenticity, and output-contract consistency can regress silently. Pass 3 is the single semantic boundary per T0-HIST-M35; unified reasoning does not relocate invariants to Pass 3 or earlier.
violation_patterns.description: Pass 3 assemble.ts invariants weakened or bypassed to accommodate the unified response.

does_not_apply_when: Operational use of IRA outputs by humans or downstream agents (those use the output contract)
Modifying other agent prompt templates (use entries 10, 11, 12)

prompt_shape_detail.scale: Ambient full-body + density identifier_only produces ~42K input tokens at a representative fingerprint (2026-04-19 measurement). Density at density_summary / abbreviated / full_body grows with signal accumulation but is bounded by CRL promotion. Well within existing IRA MAX_TOKENS.
prompt_shape_detail.description: The IRA runtime builds the prompt directly from the DS-021 §5.1 SurfaceManifest (ambient + density + fingerprint) rather than from a reduced SubstrateEntry[] of id/tier/title. Substrate body content is rendered inline per the fidelity the surface assembler assigned to each entry. The Phase C compatibility shim surfaceToSubstrateEntries is retired; no env flag governs the switch.
prompt_shape_detail.korean_test: Field selection is structural (by JSONB field name and tier), not language-specific content matching.
prompt_shape_detail.null_safety: All JSONB field accesses are optional-chained. Missing fields degrade gracefully — the builder never fails on shape variance across substrate tiers.
prompt_shape_detail.carry_everything: Below-promoted density entries remain visible at identifier_only fidelity; the reasoning plane may escalate any surfaced entry via signal write per DS-021 §5.4. Nothing is hidden.
retirement_triggers: Compatibility shim src/lib/agents/ira/surface_shim.ts deleted in OB-IGF-26 Phase A
Env flag IRA_USE_SURFACE_AS_PRIMARY retired — surface is the sole reasoning-plane input
PROMPT_VERSION bumped from ira-v4.1-multipass-2026-04-13 to ira-v5.0-manifest-2026-04-19

fidelity_field_mapping.full_body: statement, applies_when, escalates_when, violation_patterns, does_not_apply_when, false_positive_triggers, provenance.cross_references
fidelity_field_mapping.identifier_only: id, tier, title, and one_line when content preserves it (Carry Everything)

runtime_shape_detail.description: The IRA runtime performs one structured LLM call consuming the full DS-021 SurfaceManifest (ambient + density with fidelity-driven body rendering from Phase A buildIRAPrompt), producing the complete output contract (applicable_entries, excluded_candidates, option_recommendations, supersession_candidates, possible_gaps, plus tier_verdict and reasoning) in one tool-use response. Pass 1 / Pass 2 serial pipeline is retired.
runtime_shape_detail.signal_fidelity: Classification and Comprehension signals record per-entry fidelity from the SurfaceManifest, not from a constant-return helper. fidelityAtInitialFilter() retired in R1 Phase D.
runtime_shape_detail.assembly_boundary: src/lib/agents/ira/passes/assemble.ts — Pass 3 fail-loud invariants unchanged; continues to enforce T1-E903 tier-verdict authority, fingerprint authenticity, output-contract consistency

decomposition_mechanism.trigger: CRL-read of prior-invocation outcomes at same fingerprint (prompt_version LIKE ira-v6%)
decomposition_mechanism.decision: binary: any prior failure at this fingerprint promotes subsequent invocations to decomposed; otherwise single
decomposition_mechanism.structure: parallel-structural — four concurrent LLM calls via Promise.all, each receiving the full manifest, each constrained to emit one branch via a dedicated tool schema. No serial dependencies between branches.
decomposition_mechanism.thresholds: zero developer-assigned; all thresholds CRL-read or tier-structural per DS-021 v2 §4.2
decomposition_mechanism.cold_start_prior: tier-structural — single call for all scope categories (resolution/advisory/verification/capture)

historical_statements.wording: v4 operating_loop with 9 entries (Step 0 + Steps 1-8); no execution_architecture; no invariant_placement_strategy; pre-multi-pass substrate state - superseded by v5 when HF-IGF-04 multi-pass runtime gained substrate governance

v5 (OB-IGF-15 2026-04-13) — multi-pass execution architecture; invariant_placement_strategy; SubstrateEntry[] prompt-input shape inherited from pre-OB-IGF-25 era. Prompt shape superseded by v6 when OB-IGF-26 routed full SurfaceManifest body content to the reasoning plane.

v6 (OB-IGF-26 R0 Phase A, 2026-04-19) — retired Phase C compatibility shim; manifest body content delivered via buildIRAPrompt at Pass 1. Pass 2 per-unit extractors retained and were the second reduction layer R0 Phase B diagnosed. Superseded by v7 when R1 retired the Pass 2 construct entirely.

passes.name: Identify
passes.scope: single LLM call enumerating units for Pass 2

passes.name: Per-unit extraction
passes.scope: one LLM call per unit (applicable entry, option, principle-rule pair, gap area); parallelized within PASS_2_CONCURRENCY bound; retry-isolated per unit
passes.output_contract: array of unit extractions or UnitFailure records

passes.name: Assembly
passes.scope: pure function; no LLM call; applies all fail-loud invariants

execution_architecture.provenance: HF-IGF-04 port of VP-validated pattern per ViaLuce_AI_ML_Architecture_Briefing.docx Chunked Pipeline Design Backlog 7.11

token_budget_gate.timing: fires before Pass 1 if question + Surface + always-loaded Tier 0/1 exceeds INPUT_TOKEN_BUDGET_APPROX

step_to_pass_mapping.step_0_step_0p5: execute before Pass 1; deterministic; may short-circuit

partial_failure_policy.authority: T0-E03 Comprehensive - degraded briefs below completeness threshold are unreliable
partial_failure_policy.threshold: Pass 2 unit failure rate > 25% of total units

false_positive_triggers: An IRA brief that returns more entries than necessary is verbose, not violating — Efficiency is the relevant gate (Entry 1)
An IRA escalation due to genuine substrate gap is correct behavior, not failure

Coherence findings against rules with high IVA-honored signal across many work classes are unlikely to need supersession; the disposition reaffirm_as_exception is correct when the case is exceptional rather than signaling broader rule degradation
Coherence findings during architecture redirect (T5-E961 territory) are expected behavior — the redirect IS the principle assertion, the prior rule IS the candidate for supersession
An empty supersession_candidates array is correct output when no under-service is detected; IRA must not invent findings to populate the field
An empty option_recommendations array with evaluation_status='did_not_fire' is correct output when work description and substrate present no options

An empty option_recommendations array with evaluation_status='fired_no_options' is correct output when Step 8 fired but found no substrate-grounded options to evaluate; IRA must not invent options to populate the field

Per-option coherence_findings that do not propagate to top-level supersession_candidates are correct when the finding is option-local (option conflicts with specific entry) rather than cross-cutting (principle-rule under-service)

invariant_placement_strategy.rationale: T0-HIST-M35 mandates authoritative state enforcement and verification convenience remain structurally separated. Pass 3 is the single semantic boundary where the T0-E09 output contract materializes; it is the only correct location for contract-enforcing invariants.

convenience_layer.scope: early-detection convenience that prevents cascading per-unit work; structurally subordinate to Pass 3
convenience_layer.authority: MUST NOT be treated as authoritative by downstream code; Pass 3 invariants are redundantly enforced even if Pass 1 passes

permitted_patterns: Pass 3 invariants enforce the T0-E09 output contract
Pass 1 convenience invariants catch obvious contract violations before expensive Pass 2
Schema validation at each LLM response boundary (input parsing) - this is not invariant enforcement, it is input validation
Token budget gate before Pass 1 - pre-flight discipline, not contract enforcement
prohibited_patterns: Dedicated ValidationBoundary module called from both Pass 1 and Pass 3 - conflates authoritative and convenience layers (T0-HIST-M35 violation)
Invariants in runtime.ts orchestrator - bypasses Pass 3 semantic boundary
Per-Pass-2-unit invariants that replicate Pass 3 checks - redundant computation without proportionate safety gain (T0-E01 efficiency)

content.agent_boundary_protocol_position: IRA NEVER modifies entries. IRA NEVER proposes new entries. IRA is read-only on the substrate. IRA writes only to agent_invocations (its own invocation records).

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'Given a description of intended work, the IRA returns the precise applicable intelligence as a structured brief.' The applies_when includes: 'Reviewing IRA brief output for correctness.' This is an IRA invocation performing substrate-coherence review with option evaluation. The entry's violation pattern '[critical] IRA returns a rule as binding without examining whether it still serves the higher-tier principle it instantiates for the specific work (Step 7 omitted)' directly governs this invocation — Step 7 coherence check must be performed on the G7/G11 principles against their lower-tier instantiations. The entry also governs Step 8: '[critical] IRA returns brief with options present in work description but option_recommendations field empty' — the architect has supplied two options requiring evaluation.

ip_entries.title: E/E/C/I Tetrad — Efficacy Dimension

content.name: E/E/C/I Tetrad — Efficacy
content.one_line: The IGF must resolve the right intelligence, not just any intelligence.
content.statement: The IGF must resolve the right intelligence, not just any intelligence. The brief produced by the IRA must contain the entries actually applicable to the work, ranked by priority, with reasoning that explains why each entry applies. The adherence report produced by the IVA must catch real violations and not produce false positives that train users to ignore reports. Efficacy is measured by: precision (entries returned that are applicable), recall (entries applicable that are returned), correctness of priority ranking, accuracy of violation detection, accuracy of adherence detection.
applies_when: Evaluating any IRA brief for correctness
Evaluating any IVA adherence report for correctness
Tuning the agents' applicability reasoning
Reviewing precision and recall metrics from the IMA
Evaluating whether a substrate entry is being correctly invoked
escalates_when: Brief or report correctness cannot be measured directly and requires human verification
Precision and recall metrics conflict with subjective user assessment

adherence_patterns.description: Brief precision approaches 1.0 — every entry returned is genuinely applicable
Brief recall approaches 1.0 — every applicable entry is returned
Adherence report findings have high agreement with subsequent human verification
Priority ranking matches the order users actually consult the brief

violation_patterns.consequence: Work conforms to the wrong principles. CC builds correct code that violates uncited principles.
violation_patterns.description: IRA brief returns the wrong entries (false positives) or omits applicable entries (false negatives)

violation_patterns.consequence: Users learn to ignore reports. The IVA becomes noise.
violation_patterns.description: IVA adherence report flags non-violations as violations

violation_patterns.consequence: The CLT-14B failure mode repeats: code is approved that violates principles never invoked.
violation_patterns.description: IVA adherence report misses real violations
Priority ranking in a brief is incorrect — high-priority entries ranked low or vice versa

does_not_apply_when: Evaluating coverage breadth (that is Comprehensive, Entry 3)
false_positive_triggers: An IRA brief that returns more entries than necessary is verbose, not necessarily inefficacious — Efficiency is the relevant gate
An IVA report that escalates rather than declares a violation is acting correctly when evidence is insufficient

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'The brief produced by the IRA must contain the entries actually applicable to the work, ranked by priority, with reasoning that explains why each entry applies.' Efficacy applies because this is a high-stakes disposition question — the architect is asking IRA to ground a magnitude assessment (severe vs blocking) in substrate evidence. The entry's violation pattern '[critical] IRA brief returns the wrong entries (false positives) or omits applicable entries (false negatives)' is directly relevant: the G7 and G11 commitment texts are referenced by the architect but are at identifier_only fidelity in the density tier, creating a false-negative risk that must be flagged.

ip_entries.title: E/E/C/I Tetrad — Comprehensive Dimension

content.name: E/E/C/I Tetrad — Comprehensive
content.one_line: The IGF must cover the full body of governing intelligence; gaps must be tracked, not silent.
content.statement: The IGF must cover the full body of governing intelligence. Gaps in the substrate are silent failure surfaces — work that touches a topic the substrate does not cover gets no brief, gets no verification, and the absence is invisible. Comprehensiveness is measured by: substrate coverage of known intelligence sources (Decisions, Standing Rules, Anti-Patterns, DS specs, TMR addenda, Concept Registry, memory rules), identified-and-tracked gaps (entries that should exist but do not — first-class metadata, not silent absence), and the rate at which new intelligence is captured into the substrate as it emerges in development.
applies_when: Evaluating substrate coverage against known sources of governing intelligence
Designing the ICA's harvest behavior to ensure new intelligence is captured
Reviewing gap tracking — entries that should exist but do not
Onboarding a new domain or area of platform behavior to substrate governance
Auditing whether the substrate has become a partial map
escalates_when: A gap is identified but its resolution would require tier reorganization
Comprehensive and Efficiency are in tension (more coverage = more cost) and the resolution is not obvious

adherence_patterns.description: Every known intelligence source is covered by at least one substrate entry
Identified gaps are first-class entries with status 'gap_known' rather than silent absences
ICA Mode 4 captures new intelligence at the moment of generation, not weeks later
Substrate coverage rate keeps pace with platform evolution

violation_patterns.consequence: The substrate becomes a partial map. Drift accumulates in the ungoverned regions.
violation_patterns.description: Areas of platform behavior governed by intelligence that exists nowhere in the substrate

violation_patterns.consequence: The Meta-1 workaround drift pattern from the handoff repeats at the substrate level.
violation_patterns.description: Gaps tracked silently in conversation but never recorded as substrate metadata

violation_patterns.consequence: C-13 staleness pattern repeats — the substrate falls behind reality.
violation_patterns.description: Intelligence sources (Decisions, Standing Rules, etc.) added to the platform but never migrated into substrate entries

does_not_apply_when: Evaluating the cost of operations (Efficiency, Entry 1)
false_positive_triggers: Coverage of low-value or deprecated intelligence is not required — Comprehensive applies to live, governing intelligence
A gap that has been explicitly accepted by Andrew as out of scope is not a Comprehensive failure

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'Gaps in the substrate are silent failure surfaces — work that touches a topic the substrate does not cover gets no brief, gets no verification, and the absence is invisible.' The architect explicitly requests bindings to DS-021 v1.0 Section 6 (G7/G11 commitment text), Section 12 (Substrate Bounded Authority), and Section 14 FLI-1 (G11 enforcement mechanism). These are not present as full-body ambient entries. The violation pattern '[critical] Areas of platform behavior governed by intelligence that exists nowhere in the substrate' applies: the G7/G11 enforcement mechanism specifications and substrate-bounded-authority methodology are governing intelligence for this disposition but are not surfaced at reasoning fidelity.

ip_entries.title: The Korean Test (Unifying Meta-Principle)

content.origin: Base TMR OB-24 R9 (thought experiment). Addendum 10 / Decision 66 (automated grep enforcement). Memory AP-25 (structural heuristics). CC_STANDING_RULES v3.0 (language strings). DS-014 §2.4 (capability vocabulary). Decision 24 implied (calculation primitives).
content.rationale: The exemplar case for why the governing principle tier exists. Six or seven distinct scope expressions across documents with no single document capturing the unifying meta-principle. The inventory designates this as the anchor case and the canonical proof that vialuce has an unwritten meta-layer. Requires its own focused session per handoff Section 8.
content.statement: If you replaced every field name, component name, plan name, and data_type with Korean equivalents, would the platform still produce correct results? All field identification must use structural heuristics (value ranges, data types, column distributions), never field-name matching in any language. Zero language-specific or domain-specific string literals in foundational code.
content.disposition_note: The inventory recommends this not be dispositioned without the focused Meta-Principle Architecture session scoped in handoff Section 8. Capturing here to prevent further silent drift while the focused session is scheduled.
content.drift_assessment: HIGHEST in inventory. 6-7 distinct scope expressions, no unifying statement, AP-25 numbering collision.

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'If you replaced every field name, component name, plan name, and data_type with Korean equivalents, would the platform still produce correct results? All field identification must use structural heuristics (value ranges, data types, column distributions), never field-name matching in any language.' The audit evidence (S-SCHEMA-G7-01) shows signal_type column is TEXT with no CHECK constraint. Live signal_type values use ad hoc naming (sci:classification_outcome_v2, training:plan_interpretation, sci:cost_event) rather than the three-level vocabulary (classification:/comprehension:/convergence:). The convergence service uses underscore form (convergence_calculation_validation) rather than colon prefix. This is not directly a Korean Test violation but the structural heuristic principle applies: the signal surface's type vocabulary is not structurally enforced, meaning any consumer must pattern-match on string content rather than structural constraints.

ip_entries.title: Prove, Don't Describe

content.origin: Base TMR (Prove, Don't Describe) + Base TMR (Immutable Proof Gates, OB-09/OB-14). Combined here as they form one governing commitment with two faces: evidence required (Prove) and criteria immutable (Proof Gates).
content.rationale: Upstream of the most populated principle cluster in the inventory (Cluster 3). Standing Rule 27, Rule 44, Decision 154, the CC-UAT Dual-Trace methodology, Five Layers of Proof, 100% Reconciliation Gate, and the entire Standing Rules + Anti-Pattern Registry system descend from this principle and its companion Immutable Proof Gates.
content.statement: Every number must be traceable to its source data cell. Nothing is asserted without an evidence chain. Criteria are defined before work begins and cannot be changed during execution.
content.drift_assessment: MEDIUM. Principle survived across multiple operational rules but the name and unifying statement are gone from memory. Rules express it without crediting it.

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'Every number must be traceable to its source data cell. Nothing is asserted without an evidence chain. Criteria are defined before work begins and cannot be changed during execution.' The audit operates on code-and-schema evidence with explicit criteria (probe specifications from DIAG-DS021-Phase4). The architect's note that 'runtime confirmation is deferred pending populated proof-data environment' is consistent with this principle — the disposition must be grounded in the evidence actually collected, not in assertions about runtime behavior. The IRA's recommendation must weight code-and-schema evidence accordingly, as the architect instructs.

ip_entries.title: Eight-Tier Substrate Authority Structure

content.name: Eight-Tier Substrate Authority Structure
content.one_line: Substrate is organized in eight tiers; higher tiers have higher authority; new tiers cannot be added without out-of-band protocol.
content.statement: The substrate is organized in eight tiers. The tier order matters — higher tiers have higher authority. When two entries conflict, the higher-tier entry wins. When an entry references another entry, the reference must be to an entry of equal or higher tier (lower-tier entries cannot constrain higher-tier entries). The tier structure is part of the bootstrap layer. New tiers cannot be added without an explicit out-of-band protocol. Entries can be added to existing tiers freely under normal IRA/IVA/IMA operation.
applies_when: Designing new substrate entries (which tier?)
Resolving conflicts between two entries (which tier wins?)
Validating cross-references (does the reference point upward in the tier graph?)
Considering whether to add a new tier (must invoke bootstrap modification protocol)
Auditing substrate organization for tier authority violations
escalates_when: Two entries of the same tier conflict and neither dominates
A proposed change would require tier reorganization (escalate to Entry 8 — bootstrap modification protocol)

tier_definitions.name: Bootstrap Core (Immutable)
tier_definitions.description: Minimum set of entries that define what the IGF is and how it operates. Includes E/E/C/I tetrad, eight-tier structure, supersession protocol, IMA governing principles, agent prompt templates. Cannot be modified by any agent. Modification requires Andrew's explicit out-of-band approval. Small by design — 12-22 entries at maturity.

tier_definitions.name: Governing Principles
tier_definitions.description: Meta-principles that govern multiple specific decisions. Decisions 123 (Compliance is Architecture) and 124 (Research-Derived Design) are the proof-of-concept. Layer invoked first by IRA when resolving briefs. Promotion to Tier 1 requires IMA candidate surfacing, IVA pattern verification, Andrew's explicit promotion.

tier_definitions.name: Locked Decisions
tier_definitions.description: The Decision register (Decisions 1-157, with known gaps). Each Decision is a specific architectural commitment with number, lock date, supersession status, provenance. Status values preserved: LOCKED, WITHDRAWN, DEFERRED, SUPERSEDED-PENDING VERIFICATION, DUPLICATE, ARCHIVED, ABANDONED, etc.

tier_definitions.name: Design Specifications
tier_definitions.description: DS specs (DS-001 through DS-019 plus unnumbered Tier 3 architectural specs). Each DS is detailed technical architecture for a domain. References Decisions implemented and Governing Principles invoked. DS status (DRAFT vs LOCKED) is first-class metadata.

tier_definitions.name: Concept Registry
tier_definitions.description: Foundational architectural building blocks (C-01 through C-64+). Two-Tier Agent Architecture, 9 Structural Primitives, Synaptic State, Field Identity, etc. Known gap (C-41 through C-64+) tracked as first-class metadata.

tier_definitions.name: Standing Rules and Operational Rules
tier_definitions.description: Standing Rules 1-51+, CC_STANDING_RULES Section A principles, Architecture Decision Gate, Pre-Prompt Compliance Directive, CC Diagnostic Protocol, Completion Report Enforcement. Most volatile tier — emerges from CC failure patterns and CLT findings constantly.

tier_definitions.name: Anti-Pattern Registry and Failure Pattern Registry
tier_definitions.description: CC Anti-Pattern Registry (AP-1 through AP-25+) and CC Failure Pattern catalogue (FP-49 through FP-114+). Each entry is a specific mistake with the correct pattern and the source CLT/HF/OB where it was first identified.

tier_definitions.name: Methodology Reference (TMR + Addenda)
tier_definitions.description: Base TMR plus 10 Addenda. Read-mostly after bootstrap. New methodology insights become Tier 1 Governing Principle candidates, Tier 2 Decisions, or Tier 5 Standing Rules. Tier 7 preserves historical context, origin stories, biological analogies.

tier_definitions.name: Reference and Ground Truth
tier_definitions.description: Schema references, pricing models, ground truth files (BCL, CRP, Meridian), CLT findings registries, persistent defect registries. Factual ground truth that other tiers reference. Not architectural authority.

adherence_patterns.description: Cross-references go upward in the tier graph: lower tiers reference higher tiers as authority, not the reverse
Conflicts between entries are resolved by tier order — higher tier wins
Tier additions go through the bootstrap modification protocol with E/E/C/I justification

violation_patterns.consequence: The tier authority graph is broken. Substrate consistency is compromised.
violation_patterns.description: A lower-tier entry that constrains or contradicts a higher-tier entry
A cross-reference that points downward in the tier graph (e.g., a Tier 1 entry that depends on a Tier 5 entry for authority)
Adding a new tier without invoking the bootstrap modification protocol

does_not_apply_when: Operational content correctness within a tier (use the relevant tier's governing entries)
content.tier_authority_graph: Tier 0 governs → Tier 1 instantiated by → Tier 2 implemented by → Tier 3 elaborates → Tier 4 enforced by → Tier 5 defended by → Tier 6 ↑ historical context from Tier 7 ↑ factual grounding from Tier 8
false_positive_triggers: An entry referencing another entry of the same tier is allowed — the constraint is on lower tiers referencing for authority

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'The substrate is organized in eight tiers. The tier order matters — higher tiers have higher authority. When two entries conflict, the higher-tier entry wins.' This binds because the disposition depends on entries at multiple tiers: T1 principles (E906, E902, E912) govern the architectural intent, while T2+ density entries (Decision 64, Decision 23, Decision 30, Decision 77) likely contain the specific G7/G11 commitment text. The tier authority structure determines that T1 principles take precedence over T2 implementation decisions if they conflict, which is material to the (a) vs (b) disposition.

ip_entries.title: E/E/C/I Tetrad — Joint Constraint Meta-Entry

content.name: E/E/C/I Tetrad — Joint Constraint
content.one_line: E, E, C, and I are not independent; they trade off against each other and must be evaluated as a joint constraint.
content.statement: E, E, C, and I are not independent. Each one trades off against the others under specific conditions. Pushing Efficiency too hard (smaller brief, fewer tokens) reduces Efficacy and Comprehensive. Pushing Comprehensive too hard (every entry returned for every query) reduces Efficiency. Pushing Innovate too hard without Efficacy guardrails produces speculative changes that destabilize the substrate. The IGF spec does not optimize one dimension at the expense of another. It maintains the joint constraint. Every design choice is evaluated against all four. The bootstrap layer prohibits any IRA/IVA/IMA action that would violate the joint constraint. The IMA's first responsibility is detecting when an IGF operation has degraded one dimension below an acceptable threshold and surfacing that for human review. The tetrad applies recursively. The IRA/IVA/IMA prompts themselves must satisfy E/E/C/I. The substrate schema must satisfy E/E/C/I. The version control protocol must satisfy E/E/C/I. The integration points must satisfy E/E/C/I. There is no place in the IGF that escapes the tetrad.
applies_when: Evaluating any proposed change against the tetrad
Reviewing tradeoffs between two or more tetrad dimensions
Designing IMA monitoring for tetrad degradation
Resolving conflicts between agents that prioritize different dimensions
Auditing whether a substrate operation satisfies the joint constraint or just one dimension
escalates_when: Two dimensions are in unresolvable tension and a tradeoff decision must be made by Andrew
The joint constraint would require modification to be satisfied (escalate to Entry 8 — bootstrap modification protocol)

adherence_patterns.description: Every substrate change includes explicit E/E/C/I evaluation in its justification
IMA monitors all four dimensions and surfaces degradation in any one
Tradeoffs between dimensions are made explicit and dispositioned, not silently accepted
The joint constraint applies recursively to the IGF's own operations and substrate schema

violation_patterns.consequence: The joint constraint is silently violated. The substrate becomes optimized along one axis at the expense of others.
violation_patterns.description: A substrate change that improves one dimension while degrading another below threshold, without explicit justification
An IRA/IVA/IMA operation that would prioritize one dimension over the joint constraint
A substrate operation that does not evaluate all four dimensions before commit
An IMA innovation opportunity that improves Innovate without measurable improvement in E, E, or C

does_not_apply_when: A single tetrad dimension is being evaluated in isolation for analysis purposes (use Entries 1-4 directly)
false_positive_triggers: Temporary degradation of one dimension during a planned experiment is acceptable if the experiment has clear E/E/C/I justification
Initial substrate population work may have uneven E/E/C/I profile; the joint constraint applies to steady-state operation

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'E, E, C, and I are not independent. Each one trades off against the others under specific conditions.' The disposition between option (a) and option (b) is fundamentally a joint-constraint evaluation: option (a) is more Efficient (less rework) but may be less Efficacious (if in-place remediation cannot achieve full G7/G11 coherence); option (b) is more Efficacious (clean architecture) but less Efficient (rebuild cost). The entry states: 'Every design choice is evaluated against all four. The bootstrap layer prohibits any IRA/IVA/IMA action that would violate the joint constraint.' The IRA must evaluate both options against all four tetrad dimensions.

ip_entries.title: Choose Right Over Quick

content.one_line: When in doubt between a quick solution and a correct solution, always choose correct.
content.statement: When facing a choice between a quick solution and a correct solution, always choose correct. The cost of redesign at decision time is always less than the cost of fixing an incorrect solution after deployment. This principle resolves all ambiguity in favor of architectural correctness over implementation speed. It is non-negotiable and applies to every architectural decision without exception. 'Quick' is not a valid architectural justification. Speed of delivery is a constraint to be managed, not a criterion that overrides correctness. When a quick solution is also correct, there is no conflict. When they diverge, correctness wins. This principle does not prohibit iteration or incremental delivery — it prohibits choosing a known-incorrect approach because it is faster.

provenance.origin_section: SECTION D: CC OPERATIONAL RULES — Architecture — Rule 22

applies_when: Facing a tradeoff between implementation speed and architectural correctness
Evaluating whether a shortcut or workaround is acceptable
Reviewing whether a solution was chosen for speed over correctness
Resolving disagreement between delivery timeline pressure and architectural requirements
Any architectural decision where the correct path requires more time than the quick path
escalates_when: A quick solution chosen over a correct one requires redesign after deployment, confirming the principle was violated
A pattern of speed-over-correctness decisions accumulates into systemic architectural debt
A standing rule is violated and the justification given is delivery timeline pressure
cross_references: Stop and Redesign on Any Principle Violation
Anti-Pattern Registry Check Required for Every OB/HF
Fix Logic, Not Data (CC Standing Rule)
Bearer-token middleware bypass is a deflection mechanism with documented sunset trigger
Meta-33: Paper Discipline Without Enforcement Teeth Is Convention, Not Architecture
adherence_patterns: Architectural decisions are documented with explicit justification for why the chosen approach is correct, not just fast
Workarounds that are accepted for speed include a documented sunset trigger and escalation path
Timeline pressure is escalated to the architect rather than resolved by choosing an incorrect approach
Post-delivery reviews check whether speed-driven decisions introduced correctness debt

violation_patterns.description: A known-incorrect architectural approach is chosen because it is faster to implement, with the intent to fix it later
A shortcut is taken that violates a standing architectural rule, justified by delivery timeline pressure
A 'temporary' workaround is implemented without a documented sunset trigger, effectively making it permanent
Speed of delivery is cited as a reason to skip the Anti-Pattern Registry check or Architecture Decision Gate

false_positive_triggers: Choosing a simpler correct solution over a more complex correct solution is not a violation — simplicity and correctness are not in conflict
Incremental delivery of a correct solution is not a violation — the principle targets incorrect approaches, not incomplete ones

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'When facing a choice between a quick solution and a correct solution, always choose correct. The cost of redesign at decision time is always less than the cost of fixing an incorrect solution after deployment.' This principle directly governs the (a) vs (b) disposition. If option (a) is the 'quick' path that cannot achieve full architectural correctness, and option (b) is the 'correct' path that requires more work, this principle resolves in favor of (b). However, the principle also states: 'Choosing a simpler correct solution over a more complex correct solution is not a violation — simplicity and correctness are not in conflict.' If option (a) can achieve correctness, it is not the 'quick over correct' pattern.

ip_entries.title: VP-to-VG Supersession Propagation

content.name: VP-to-VG Supersession Propagation

health.verification_evidence: Originated as supersession_candidate in IRA invocation BMK-IRA-001. T0-E09 under-serves T0-E12 because no rule propagates VP supersession events to VG runtime implementations.

content.one_line: When VP supersedes a pattern, VG runtime implementing that pattern must adopt the superseding design, document a justified exception, or capture data toward the superseding design.
content.statement: VP governance archive contains supersession events — locked Decisions that explicitly replace prior architectural patterns (e.g., Decision 110 CRR superseding SCI competitive scoring; Decision 111 superseding earlier Field Identity approaches; Decision 64 superseding pre-Dual-Intelligence patterns). When VG implements a runtime instantiating the same architectural family as a VP-superseded pattern, the VG implementation must honor the supersession via one of: (1) ADOPT — implement the superseding VP design directly; (2) EXCEPT — document a justified, time-bound exception with concrete revisit trigger (e.g., 'deferred until VG corpus reaches N entries'); (3) PREPARE — implement an interim design that captures data in the shape the superseding pattern will consume. Re-instantiating the exact pattern that VP superseded — without exception documentation, without preparation toward the superseding design — violates this principle.

provenance.origin_artifact: IRA invocation BMK-IRA-001 baseline response at docs/IRA-responses/IRA_OB_IGF_20_P0_CRR_Resolver_Choice_20260414.md

applies_when: Drafting any VG agent runtime that instantiates a VP architectural pattern
Designing VG resolution layers, classification pipelines, or scoring runtimes
Reviewing OB/HF specs for runtime architecture choices when VP has prior art
Auditing whether VG implementations honor VP supersession events
Selecting between architectural options where one or more re-instantiate a VP-superseded pattern
escalates_when: VP supersession event itself is contested or pending verification
Multiple VP supersession events on the same pattern family produce conflicting guidance
VG runtime would require Tier 0 amendment to honor supersession (escalates to T0-E08 bootstrap modification protocol)

adherence_patterns.description: OB/HF spec drafting includes a VP supersession check step
When VP supersession history exists, the spec explicitly states ADOPT / EXCEPT / PREPARE choice and why
EXCEPT-path choices include concrete time-bound trigger condition tracked as a benchmark
PREPARE-path choices include explicit data-shape contract for the future superseding pattern
Mapping tables claiming 'same as VP pattern X' have implementation matching the cited pattern, not the pre-supersession ancestor

violation_patterns.consequence: VG re-instantiates the failure mode VP locked governance against. Future migration requires teardown.
violation_patterns.description: VG runtime implements a pattern that VP has explicitly superseded without exception documentation or PREPARE-pattern data capture

violation_patterns.consequence: Documentation drift; mapping tables cannot be trusted to reflect implementation.
violation_patterns.description: Spec mapping table claims structural parity with a superseding VP pattern while implementation encodes the superseded pattern

violation_patterns.consequence: EXCEPT path chosen but PREPARE benefits unrealized.
violation_patterns.description: VG runtime defers to architect for resolution but does NOT capture the data shape the superseding pattern needs

does_not_apply_when: Implementing tooling, schemas, or infrastructure with no VP architectural ancestor
Implementing a VP pattern that VP itself has not superseded
Implementing primitive utilities tier-orthogonal to architectural-pattern decisions
false_positive_triggers: VP architectural pattern with no supersession history — no propagation obligation arises
Pattern family difference too large — VG context legitimately requires different architecture; documenting why is principle adherence
Cold-start pragmatism cited explicitly as EXCEPT path with revisit trigger is principle adherence
PREPARE-path implementation that captures data toward superseding pattern but does not yet swap resolver is principle adherence

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'When VG implements a runtime instantiating the same architectural family as a VP-superseded pattern, the VG implementation must honor the supersession via one of: (1) ADOPT, (2) EXCEPT, (3) PREPARE.' The audit evidence shows the current signal surface architecture may instantiate a pattern that VP governance (DS-021 Decision 64 v2, three-level signal_type vocabulary) has superseded. The violation pattern '[critical] VG runtime implements a pattern that VP has explicitly superseded without exception documentation or PREPARE-pattern data capture' is relevant: if the current bypass-channel architecture and ad hoc signal_type vocabulary represent a pre-supersession pattern, the disposition must account for supersession propagation obligations.

ip_entries.title: Governance Enforces Actors, Not Curates Substrate

content.origin: Session 2026-04-12, Enforcer reframe discussion. Architect named original conception as actor governance in founding vision; Claude drifted to substrate-curation framing across multiple sessions; reframe locked this session. Synaptic surface metaphor selected over gate to defeat CC tendency to orient toward getting-through verification rather than gaining from it.
content.statement: Governance is the function of constraining actor behavior against a rule set, not the function of curating the rule set itself. Substrate is the constraint set; agents are the enforcement mechanism; the architect-Claude-CC pipeline is the surface being enforced. A queryable rule that the actor can ignore is theater, not governance. Substrate-curation work (capture, version, verify-internal-consistency) is supporting infrastructure for enforcement, not the product. The product is the synaptic surface between actor and artifact — every output crosses it, every crossing is verified and weighted, signal propagates only when threshold conditions are met, and the surface itself adapts to what it has seen before.
cross_references: Binds all subsequent IGF agent design: IRA serves IP for enforcement consumers, IVA verifies actor output against served IP, IMA monitors enforcement system. Aligns with Synaptic Surface naming in Enforcer v0 spec; subsequent architectural language adopts synaptic vocabulary (verify, weight, threshold-fire, adapt) rather than gate vocabulary (admit, reject, pass-through).

ip_entries.why_it_binds: Statement: 'Governance is the function of constraining actor behavior against a rule set, not the function of curating the rule set itself.' And: 'A queryable rule that the actor can ignore is theater, not governance.' The audit evidence shows G7 and G11 exist as governance commitments but the implementation structurally cannot comply: the signal surface is write-only (actors write signals but no actor reads them back through the canonical surface), and bypass channels allow actors to circumvent the canonical surface entirely. This is the 'queryable rule that the actor can ignore' pattern — G7/G11 exist as rules but the architecture does not enforce them.

supersession_candidates.coherence_finding: IGF-T2-E29 (Decision 30: Classification Signals Terminology) is at identifier_only fidelity and cannot be body-cited. However, the audit evidence shows the classification_signals table's signal_type vocabulary does not use the three-level prefix system (classification:/comprehension:/convergence:) that DS-021/Decision 64 v2 committed to. If Decision 30 defines the terminology that the signal surface should use, and the live implementation uses ad hoc naming (sci:classification_outcome_v2, training:plan_interpretation, sci:cost_event), then Decision 30 may need supersession or reaffirmation against the live evidence. IGF-T1-E906 (Closed-Loop Intelligence) requires signals to 'accumulate into the platform's learning systems' — if the terminology is inconsistent, signal accumulation across the three flywheels cannot be structurally coherent.
