supersession_candidates.reasoning: Decision 30 likely needs extension to account for the three-level vocabulary commitment from Decision 64 v2 and to address the live vocabulary divergence. This cannot be confirmed without escalating IGF-T2-E29 to full body fidelity. ESCALATION RECOMMENDED per DS-021 §5.4 Carry Everything: IGF-T2-E29 should be surfaced at density_summary or full_body fidelity for the next invocation touching signal surface architecture.

supersession_candidates.coherence_finding: IGF-T2-E01 (Decision 64: Dual Intelligence Architecture) is at identifier_only fidelity. The audit evidence references 'DS-021/Decision 64 v2' as the source of the three-level signal_type vocabulary (classification:/comprehension:/convergence:). If Decision 64 v2 defines the Dual Intelligence Architecture that G7 and G11 instantiate, then the live implementation's divergence (write-only signal surface, bypass channels, ad hoc signal_type vocabulary) represents a coherence gap between this T2 decision and the T1 principle (E906 Closed-Loop Intelligence). The T1 principle governs; the T2 decision should be checked for whether it still instantiates the principle given the evidence.
supersession_candidates.reasoning: Decision 64 likely contains the G7/G11 commitment text the architect requested. Without full body, the coherence check cannot be completed. ESCALATION RECOMMENDED: IGF-T2-E01 should be surfaced at full_body fidelity for this audit disposition. The architect's explicit binding request ('DS-021 v1.0 Section 6 G7 commitment text', 'DS-021 v1.0 Section 6 G11 commitment text') cannot be served at identifier_only fidelity.

supersession_candidates.coherence_finding: IGF-T2-E28 (Decision 23: Three Flywheels — Tenant, Foundational, Domain) is at identifier_only fidelity. The audit evidence shows the three flywheel tables (synaptic_density for Tenant, foundational_patterns for Foundational, domain_patterns for Domain) operate as bypass channels that do not flow through classification_signals. IGF-T1-E936 states the flywheel must not 'discard classification outcomes without feeding them back into the signal accumulation mechanism.' If Decision 23 defines the three-flywheel architecture, the coherence question is whether the flywheel tables were always intended to be separate from classification_signals (in which case G7's 'single canonical signal surface' needs reinterpretation) or whether they were intended to read from classification_signals (in which case the bypass is a coherence gap).
supersession_candidates.reasoning: This is a critical coherence question for the (a) vs (b) disposition. If the three-flywheel architecture was designed with separate tables by intent, then G7 may need reinterpretation to mean 'single canonical write surface with structured read-paths through flywheel tables' rather than 'all signals in one table.' If the separation is drift, then option (b) rebuild is clearly indicated. ESCALATION RECOMMENDED: IGF-T2-E28 at full_body fidelity would resolve this ambiguity.

option_recommendations.alignment_reasoning: Option (a) — remediable through architectural pattern change — proposes migrating 4 whole-table bypass channels to flow through classification_signals, adding run_id-equivalent column, and building cross-run read-path on convergence service. SUBSTRATE ASSESSMENT: This option faces three structural barriers that the evidence makes visible. (1) SCHEMA-IMPOSSIBILITY BARRIER: The signal surface lacks run_id/calculation_run_id/batch_id/session_id (S-SIGNAL-G11-01). Adding a run_id column is a schema migration, not a pattern change — it changes the fundamental identity model of the signal surface. This is remediable but is not 'pattern change' magnitude; it is schema-level architectural change. (2) BYPASS CHANNEL CONSOLIDATION BARRIER: The 4 bypass tables (synaptic_density, foundational_patterns, domain_patterns, structural_fingerprints) serve distinct architectural functions — per-tenant learning, cross-tenant aggregation, per-fingerprint classification reuse, column-role assignment. These are not accidental bypasses; they are purpose-built tables with distinct schemas, key structures, and query patterns (S-CODE-G7-01, S-CODE-G7-02). Migrating their content into classification_signals would require either (i) making classification_signals a polymorphic mega-table (violating domain-agnostic schema principles) or (ii) creating a view/materialized-view layer that unifies them (which is architecturally a rebuild of the read-path, not a pattern change). (3) CONVERGENCE SERVICE BARRIER: The convergence service (2104 lines) has zero .select calls against classification_signals and zero run_id references (S-CODE-G11-01a). Making it read from the signal surface is not adding a query — it requires redesigning how the convergence service discovers and consumes prior classification intelligence, which is currently done through the bypass channels. Per IGF-T1-E930 (Choose Right Over Quick): if option (a) cannot achieve full G7/G11 coherence, it is the 'quick' path and the principle resolves against it. Per IGF-T0-E05 (Joint Constraint): option (a) scores higher on Efficiency (less rework) but the evidence suggests it scores lower on Efficacy (the bypass channels serve distinct architectural purposes that cannot be trivially consolidated). Per IGF-T1-E936: the flywheel architecture (foundational_patterns, domain_patterns) is structurally separate from classification_signals by design — the flywheel reads/writes go to different tables with different schemas. Consolidating them is not a pattern change but an architectural redesign of the flywheel's data model. MAGNITUDE ASSESSMENT: The architect's framing of option (a) as 'severe' understates the evidence. The bypass channels are not accidental drift — they are purpose-built architectural components. 'Migrating' them is closer to rebuilding them.

conflicts_detected.reasoning: If option (a) cannot achieve full G7/G11 coherence — and the evidence suggests the bypass channels serve distinct purposes that resist consolidation into a single table — then choosing option (a) would be choosing quick over correct. The principle states: 'a known-incorrect architectural approach chosen because it is faster to implement' is a critical violation.

The flywheel architecture (foundational_patterns, domain_patterns, structural_fingerprints) is the structural expression of the three-flywheel commitment (Tenant, Foundational, Domain per IGF-T1-E906). Consolidating these into classification_signals would either destroy the flywheel's distinct data model or require classification_signals to become a polymorphic surface — neither of which is a 'pattern change.'

option_recommendations.recommended_action: Option (a) as framed is NOT RECOMMENDED. The evidence does not support 'severe magnitude — coordinated remediation' as sufficient. However, a MODIFIED option (a) — where the bypass channels are preserved as purpose-built components but a read-path unification layer is built on top of them, with run_id-equivalent added to classification_signals and a convergence service read-path built — would be architecturally viable. This modified version converges toward option (b) in scope but preserves the existing bypass table schemas. The distinction between modified-(a) and (b) narrows to whether the convergence service is rebuilt from scratch or refactored in place.

coherence_findings.finding: Option (a) assumes bypass channels are accidental drift. The evidence shows they are purpose-built (distinct schemas, distinct query patterns, distinct architectural functions). The 'pattern change' framing underestimates the scope.

option_recommendations.alignment_reasoning: Option (b) — requires rebuild of convergence service / signal surface architecture — proposes that the current convergence service is write-only on the signal surface and the surface itself lacks run-scoping, making in-place coherence architecturally unreachable without rebuilding both. SUBSTRATE ASSESSMENT: The evidence strongly supports this option's premises. (1) WRITE-ONLY CONFIRMATION: S-CODE-G11-01a confirms zero .select calls against classification_signals in the convergence service. The only contact is a single INSERT at line 253. This is not a read-path gap that can be patched — the convergence service's entire architecture does not consume the signal surface. (2) SCHEMA-IMPOSSIBILITY CONFIRMATION: S-SIGNAL-G11-01 confirms no run_id, calculation_run_id, batch_id, or session_id in classification_signals. Within-run filtering is 'structurally impossible' per the audit. This is a schema-level gap requiring migration + backfill + consumer update. (3) BYPASS CHANNEL STRUCTURAL PURPOSE: S-CODE-G7-02 shows the bypass channels carry distinct JSONB vocabularies (learned_behaviors, classification_result, column_roles) serving distinct architectural functions (cold-start priors, fingerprint reuse, column-role carriage). These are not duplicates of classification_signals — they are complementary surfaces that the current architecture reads INSTEAD OF classification_signals. (4) SIGNAL_TYPE VOCABULARY GAP: S-SCHEMA-G7-01 shows zero rows using the three-level vocabulary (classification:/comprehension:/convergence:) from DS-021/Decision 64 v2. The live vocabulary is ad hoc. Rebuilding the signal surface would include enforcing the committed vocabulary. Per IGF-T1-E930: option (b) is the 'correct' path. The evidence shows the current architecture cannot achieve G7/G11 coherence through pattern change alone. Per IGF-T1-E914: if the current bypass-channel architecture represents a pre-supersession pattern that VP governance (Decision 64 v2) has superseded, option (b) is the ADOPT path — implementing the superseding design directly. Per IGF-T1-E911: the current state is 'a queryable rule that the actor can ignore is theater, not governance' — G7/G11 exist as commitments but the architecture does not enforce them. Option (b) addresses this by rebuilding the enforcement surface. Per IGF-T0-E05 (Joint Constraint): option (b) scores lower on Efficiency (more rework) but higher on Efficacy (achieves actual G7/G11 coherence), higher on Comprehensive (unified signal surface enables comprehensive read-path), and higher on Innovate (rebuilt architecture can incorporate the three-level vocabulary and run-scoping from the start). MAGNITUDE ASSESSMENT: The architect's framing of option (b) as 'blocking' is supported by the evidence. The convergence service (2104 lines, write-only on signal surface, zero run_id references) and the signal surface schema (no run-scoping columns, no type vocabulary enforcement) both require rebuild, not refactoring.

conflicts_detected.reasoning: Option (b) is the most expensive path. IGF-T0-E01 states 'Efficiency is measured by: time-to-brief for the IRA, time-to-report for the IVA, total LLM tokens per resolution cycle.' While this applies to IGF operations not platform architecture, the joint constraint (E05) requires evaluating Efficiency alongside Efficacy. The rebuild cost is real but the evidence suggests it is the minimum cost to achieve coherence.

option_recommendations.recommended_action: RECOMMENDED with substrate-bounded-authority caveat. The evidence supports option (b) — blocking magnitude requiring rebuild. However, the IRA flags that this disposition PARTIALLY DEPENDS on content not yet at reasoning fidelity: (1) DS-021 v1.0 Section 14 FLI-1 (G11 enforcement mechanism specification) is forward-looking and not in the ambient substrate — the enforcement mechanism design may affect whether rebuild scope includes enforcement infrastructure or only the signal surface + convergence service. (2) DS-021 v1.0 Section 12 (Substrate Bounded Authority methodology) is not at full body fidelity — the methodology for determining what is in-scope for this disposition vs. what requires forward-looking architectural work may constrain the recommendation. (3) The bypass channels (foundational_patterns, domain_patterns, structural_fingerprints, synaptic_density) serve the three-flywheel architecture (IGF-T1-E906, IGF-T2-E28). Rebuilding the signal surface must preserve the flywheel's distinct data model while establishing a read-path that unifies signal consumption. This is architectural design work, not just schema migration. RECOMMENDED SCOPE: Rebuild classification_signals schema (add run_id-equivalent, enforce signal_type vocabulary via CHECK constraint), rebuild convergence service read-path (from zero .select to structured within-run and cross-run consumption), establish read-path unification layer over bypass channels (preserving their schemas but making their content queryable through or alongside the canonical surface), and address the migration/schema divergence finding (14 columns not in committed migrations).

coherence_findings.finding: Option (b) aligns with IGF-T1-E914 ADOPT path: if the current architecture represents a pre-supersession pattern, rebuilding to the superseding design is the principled response. The three-level signal_type vocabulary (classification:/comprehension:/convergence:) from Decision 64 v2 is not present in the current implementation (zero rows use it), supporting the supersession interpretation.

The distinction between option (a) and option (b) narrows when option (a) is honestly scoped. A 'modified option (a)' that preserves bypass table schemas but builds a unification layer + adds run_id + rebuilds convergence read-path approaches option (b) in scope. The honest question is whether the convergence service can be refactored in place (2104 lines, write-only) or must be rebuilt. The evidence (zero .select, zero run_id references, single INSERT contact) suggests rebuild is more honest than refactor.

preamble_text:
Enforcer-loaded IP for this turn:
[IGF-T1-E906] T1 "Closed-Loop Intelligence" — Statement: 'Every interaction generates a classification signal that accumulates into the platform's learning systems. The three-flywheel architecture (Tenant, Foundational, Domain) is the structural expression of this commitment.' The audit evidence shows classification_signals is write-only (convergence service has zero .select calls against it), the flywheel tables (foundational_patterns, domain_patterns, structural_fingerprints) operate as bypass channels reading/writing outside classification_signals, and the signal surface lacks run_id for within-run filtering. This directly implicates the closed-loop intelligence commitment: if signals are written but never read back, the loop is not closed. The G11 ABSENT verdict on both within-run and cross-run read-paths is a structural violation of this principle's core proposition that signals 'accumulate into the platform's learning systems.'
[IGF-T1-E902] T1 "Carry Everything, Express Contextually" — Statement: 'At import time, the platform persists ALL data regardless of whether AI has classified it. AI classifications are hints, not gates. Data is preserved at import; context activates at calculation time.' The audit evidence reveals that classification-relevant data is scattered across 4 whole-table bypass channels (synaptic_density, foundational_patterns, domain_patterns, structural_fingerprints) plus 2 JSONB per-key bypasses (import_batches.metadata.classification, import_batches.metadata.contentUnitId). The 'Carry Everything' principle requires all data to be persisted and contextually activated — but the bypass channels mean classification intelligence is carried in fragmented locations rather than through a single canonical surface. The principle's 'Express Contextually' dimension is structurally impossible when the signal surface itself cannot be queried for within-run context (no run_id column).
[IGF-T1-E912] T1 "Principle-Rule Coherence and the Supersession Surface" — Statement: 'When the substrate is queried for binding intelligence on a piece of work, two distinct findings are possible: (1) applicability — which rules govern this work — and (2) coherence — whether the rules at lower tiers still serve the principles at higher tiers, given the specific case under consideration.' This audit IS a coherence review: the architect is asking whether the lower-tier implementation (convergence service, signal surface schema, flywheel tables) still serves the higher-tier principles (G7, G11) given the code-and-schema evidence collected. The entry further states: 'Applicability without coherence produces under-service: the rule was applied, the work conformed, the principle was nonetheless degraded because the rule no longer instantiates the principle for the case at hand.' The evidence shows exactly this pattern — classification_signals exists (rule applied), writes occur (work conforms to write-path), but the principle (read-path coherence, single canonical surface) is degraded because the implementation does not instantiate the principle.
[IGF-T1-E913] T1 "IRA Returns Recommendations, Not Just Bindings" — Statement: 'When a piece of work includes options — either user-supplied (the architect names candidate paths in the work description) or substrate-grounded (substrate explicitly enumerates alternatives that apply to the work) — IRA evaluates each option against the assembled brief and surfaces a ranked recommendation with substrate-cited reasoning per option.' The architect explicitly names two options: (a) remediable through architectural pattern change and (b) requires rebuild. These are user-supplied options requiring Step 8 evaluation. The entry further states: 'IRA does not invent options not present in either source. The recommendation field is empty when no substrate-grounded options exist for evaluation.' Both options are present in the user prompt; evaluation is required.
[IGF-T1-E924] T1 "Closed-Loop Learning" — Statement: 'Every correction, override, or manual adjustment is a learning signal that must feed back into the AI interpretation layer, not be discarded after the immediate fix. Closed-loop learning is the mechanism by which the platform compounds intelligence over time; without it, every correction is a one-time patch rather than a permanent improvement.' The audit evidence shows the convergence service writes to classification_signals but never reads from it (zero .select calls). The flywheel pipeline reads from foundational_patterns and domain_patterns — bypass channels — not from classification_signals. This means the closed-loop learning mechanism bypasses the canonical signal surface entirely. The principle's core proposition — that the platform 'compounds intelligence over time' — is structurally undermined when the signal surface is write-only and the learning loop reads from bypass channels.
[IGF-T1-E936] T1 "Closed-Loop Intelligence Applied to Data Ingestion" — Statement: 'The ML flywheel spins: more data → better classification → less human intervention → faster processing → more data.' And: 'Any implementation that discards classification outcomes (human or autonomous) without feeding them back into the signal accumulation mechanism breaks the flywheel.' The audit evidence (S-CODE-G11-02) shows flywheel files (flywheel-pipeline.ts, fingerprint-flywheel.ts) have zero references to classification_signals. The flywheel reads/writes go to foundational_patterns, domain_patterns, structural_fingerprints — NOT classification_signals. This is a structural bypass: the flywheel accumulation mechanism does not consume the canonical signal surface. The principle's violation pattern '[high] A new ingestion channel (e.g., API push) does not feed classification outcomes into the same signal accumulation mechanism as SFTP, creating a split flywheel' applies by analogy — the flywheel and the signal surface are split.
[IGF-T0-E09] T0 "IRA (Intelligence Resolution Agent) Prompt Template" — Statement: 'Given a description of intended work, the IRA returns the precise applicable intelligence as a structured brief.' The applies_when includes: 'Reviewing IRA brief output for correctness.' This is an IRA invocation performing substrate-coherence review with option evaluation. The entry's violation pattern '[critical] IRA returns a rule as binding without examining whether it still serves the higher-tier principle it instantiates for the specific work (Step 7 omitted)' directly governs this invocation — Step 7 coherence check must be performed on the G7/G11 principles against their lower-tier instantiations. The entry also governs Step 8: '[critical] IRA returns brief with options present in work description but option_recommendations field empty' — the architect has supplied two options requiring evaluation.
[IGF-T0-E02] T0 "E/E/C/I Tetrad — Efficacy Dimension" — Statement: 'The brief produced by the IRA must contain the entries actually applicable to the work, ranked by priority, with reasoning that explains why each entry applies.' Efficacy applies because this is a high-stakes disposition question — the architect is asking IRA to ground a magnitude assessment (severe vs blocking) in substrate evidence. The entry's violation pattern '[critical] IRA brief returns the wrong entries (false positives) or omits applicable entries (false negatives)' is directly relevant: the G7 and G11 commitment texts are referenced by the architect but are at identifier_only fidelity in the density tier, creating a false-negative risk that must be flagged.
[IGF-T0-E03] T0 "E/E/C/I Tetrad — Comprehensive Dimension" — Statement: 'Gaps in the substrate are silent failure surfaces — work that touches a topic the substrate does not cover gets no brief, gets no verification, and the absence is invisible.' The architect explicitly requests bindings to DS-021 v1.0 Section 6 (G7/G11 commitment text), Section 12 (Substrate Bounded Authority), and Section 14 FLI-1 (G11 enforcement mechanism). These are not present as full-body ambient entries. The violation pattern '[critical] Areas of platform behavior governed by intelligence that exists nowhere in the substrate' applies: the G7/G11 enforcement mechanism specifications and substrate-bounded-authority methodology are governing intelligence for this disposition but are not surfaced at reasoning fidelity.
[IGF-T1-E910] T1 "The Korean Test (Unifying Meta-Principle)" — Statement: 'If you replaced every field name, component name, plan name, and data_type with Korean equivalents, would the platform still produce correct results? All field identification must use structural heuristics (value ranges, data types, column distributions), never field-name matching in any language.' The audit evidence (S-SCHEMA-G7-01) shows signal_type column is TEXT with no CHECK constraint. Live signal_type values use ad hoc naming (sci:classification_outcome_v2, training:plan_interpretation, sci:cost_event) rather than the three-level vocabulary (classification:/comprehension:/convergence:). The convergence service uses underscore form (convergence_calculation_validation) rather than colon prefix. This is not directly a Korean Test violation but the structural heuristic principle applies: the signal surface's type vocabulary is not structurally enforced, meaning any consumer must pattern-match on string content rather than structural constraints.
[IGF-T1-E905] T1 "Prove, Don't Describe" — Statement: 'Every number must be traceable to its source data cell. Nothing is asserted without an evidence chain. Criteria are defined before work begins and cannot be changed during execution.' The audit operates on code-and-schema evidence with explicit criteria (probe specifications from DIAG-DS021-Phase4). The architect's note that 'runtime confirmation is deferred pending populated proof-data environment' is consistent with this principle — the disposition must be grounded in the evidence actually collected, not in assertions about runtime behavior. The IRA's recommendation must weight code-and-schema evidence accordingly, as the architect instructs.
[IGF-T0-E06] T0 "Eight-Tier Substrate Authority Structure" — Statement: 'The substrate is organized in eight tiers. The tier order matters — higher tiers have higher authority. When two entries conflict, the higher-tier entry wins.' This binds because the disposition depends on entries at multiple tiers: T1 principles (E906, E902, E912) govern the architectural intent, while T2+ density entries (Decision 64, Decision 23, Decision 30, Decision 77) likely contain the specific G7/G11 commitment text. The tier authority structure determines that T1 principles take precedence over T2 implementation decisions if they conflict, which is material to the (a) vs (b) disposition.
[IGF-T0-E05] T0 "E/E/C/I Tetrad — Joint Constraint Meta-Entry" — Statement: 'E, E, C, and I are not independent. Each one trades off against the others under specific conditions.' The disposition between option (a) and option (b) is fundamentally a joint-constraint evaluation: option (a) is more Efficient (less rework) but may be less Efficacious (if in-place remediation cannot achieve full G7/G11 coherence); option (b) is more Efficacious (clean architecture) but less Efficient (rebuild cost). The entry states: 'Every design choice is evaluated against all four. The bootstrap layer prohibits any IRA/IVA/IMA action that would violate the joint constraint.' The IRA must evaluate both options against all four tetrad dimensions.
[IGF-T1-E930] T1 "Choose Right Over Quick" — Statement: 'When facing a choice between a quick solution and a correct solution, always choose correct. The cost of redesign at decision time is always less than the cost of fixing an incorrect solution after deployment.' This principle directly governs the (a) vs (b) disposition. If option (a) is the 'quick' path that cannot achieve full architectural correctness, and option (b) is the 'correct' path that requires more work, this principle resolves in favor of (b). However, the principle also states: 'Choosing a simpler correct solution over a more complex correct solution is not a violation — simplicity and correctness are not in conflict.' If option (a) can achieve correctness, it is not the 'quick over correct' pattern.
[IGF-T1-E914] T1 "VP-to-VG Supersession Propagation" — Statement: 'When VG implements a runtime instantiating the same architectural family as a VP-superseded pattern, the VG implementation must honor the supersession via one of: (1) ADOPT, (2) EXCEPT, (3) PREPARE.' The audit evidence shows the current signal surface architecture may instantiate a pattern that VP governance (DS-021 Decision 64 v2, three-level signal_type vocabulary) has superseded. The violation pattern '[critical] VG runtime implements a pattern that VP has explicitly superseded without exception documentation or PREPARE-pattern data capture' is relevant: if the current bypass-channel architecture and ad hoc signal_type vocabulary represent a pre-supersession pattern, the disposition must account for supersession propagation obligations.
[IGF-T1-E911] T1 "Governance Enforces Actors, Not Curates Substrate" — Statement: 'Governance is the function of constraining actor behavior against a rule set, not the function of curating the rule set itself.' And: 'A queryable rule that the actor can ignore is theater, not governance.'
